name: python3-watchdog
title: python3-watchdog
summary: Python API and shell utilities to monitor file system events
description:
Python API and shell utilities to monitor file system events
Watchdog comes with a utility script called watchmedo. It can be used to
execute commands in response to events.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use watchmedo because Python API and shell utilities to monitor file system events.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use watchmedo as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `watchmedo task [options]...` as documented upstream for watchmedo.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `watchmedo [option]` as documented upstream for watchmedo.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair watchmedo with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `watchmedo --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install python-watchdog. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `watchmedo task [options]...`. Python API and shell utilities to monitor file system events.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Python API and shell utilities to monitor file system events.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Python API and shell utilities to monitor file system events.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install python-watchdog
